    "Every nonstandard helper theorem or local API used in the proof should appear explicitly in the Lean prefix context or local examples. If it is not displayed, do not use its name.",

  ],
  "context": {
    "source_statement_or_chunk": [
      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/DividingFPS.tex",
        "line_range": [
          720,
          756
        ],
        "original_line_range": [
          721,

        ],
        "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
          "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
          "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
        ],
        "labels": [
          "lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna"
        ],
        "snippet": "\begin{lemma}
\label{lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna}Let $k\in\mathbb{N}$. Let $a\in K\left[
\left[  x\right]  \right]  $ be any FPS. Then, the first $k$ coefficients of
the FPS $x^{k}a$ are $0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
[Proof of Lemma \ref{lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna}.]We must show that
$\left[  x^{m}\right]  \left(  x^{k}a\right)  =0$ for any nonnegative integer
$m<k$. But we can do this directly: If $m$ is a nonnegative integer such that
$m<k$, then (\ref{pf.thm.fps.ring.xn(ab)=2}) (applied to $x^{k}$, $a$ and $m$
instead of $\mathbf{a}$, $\mathbf{b}$ and $n$) yields%
\[
\left[  x^{m}\right]  \left(  x^{k}a\right)  =\sum_{i=0}^{m}%
\underbrace{\left[  x^{i}\right]  \left(  x^{k}\right)  }%
_{\substack{=0\\\text{(since }i\leq m<k\\\text{and thus }i\neq k\text{)}%
}}\cdot\left[  x^{m-i}\right]  a=\sum_{i=0}^{m}0\cdot\left[  x^{m-i}\right]
a=0,
\]
which is exactly what we wanted to show. Thus, Lemma
\ref{lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna} is proved.

(Alternatively, we could prove Lemma \ref{lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna} by
writing $a$ in the form $a=\left(  a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},\ldots\right)  $ and
observing that $x^{k}a=\left(  \underbrace{0,0,\ldots,0}_{k\text{ times}%
},a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},\ldots\right)  $. This follows by applying Lemma
\ref{lem.fps.xa} a total of $k$ times, or more formally by induction on $k$.)
\end{proof}

Lemma \ref{lem.fps.first-n-coeffs-of-xna} has a converse; here is a statement
that combines it with this converse:

\begin{lemma}
\label{lem.fps.muls-of-xn}Let $k\in\mathbb{N}$. Let $f\in K\left[  \left[
x\right]  \right]  $ be any FPS. Then, the first $k$ coefficients of the FPS
$f$ are $0$ if and only if $f$ is a multiple of $x^{k}$.
\end{lemma}
"
      }
    ],

    "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:41-41 (open)
open scoped BigOperators

-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:43-43 (namespace)
namespace AlgebraicCombinatorics

-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:51-51 (variable)
variable {K : Type*} [CommRing K]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:328-361 (fps_onePlusX_inv)
theorem fps_onePlusX_inv {F : Type*} [Field F] :
    (1 + PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)Ōü╗┬╣ = PowerSeries.mk fun n => (-1 : F) ^ n := by
  -- (1 - X)Ōü╗┬╣ = mk 1 (the power series with all coefficients 1)
  have h1 : (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)Ōü╗┬╣ = PowerSeries.mk 1 := by
    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ŌēĀ 0 := by simp
    symm
    rw [PowerSeries.eq_inv_iff_mul_eq_one hc]
    exact PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one F
  -- rescale (-1) maps (1 - X) to (1 + X)
  have h2 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) = 1 + PowerSeries.X := by
    ext n
    simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, map_sub, map_one, PowerSeries.coeff_one]
    cases n with
    | zero => simp
    | succ n =>
      simp only [Nat.succ_ne_zero, ŌåōreduceIte, PowerSeries.coeff_X]
      split_ifs with h
      ┬Ę simp [h]
      ┬Ę have hn : n ŌēĀ 0 := by omega
        simp [PowerSeries.coeff_X, hn]
  -- rescale preserves inverses (as a ring homomorphism)
  have h3 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) ((1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)Ōü╗┬╣) =
      (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X))Ōü╗┬╣ := by
    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ŌēĀ 0 := by simp
    have hc' : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X)) ŌēĀ 0 := by
      rw [h2]; simp
    symm
    rw [PowerSeries.inv_eq_iff_mul_eq_one hc', ŌåÉ (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F)).map_mul,
        PowerSeries.inv_mul_cancel _ hc]
    simp
  -- Combine: (1+X)Ōü╗┬╣ = rescale(-1)((1-X)Ōü╗┬╣) = rescale(-1)(mk 1) = mk ((-1)^n)
  rw [ŌåÉ h2, ŌåÉ h3, h1]
  ext n
  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, PowerSeries.coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, mul_one]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:610-611 (PowerSeries.divByX)
def PowerSeries.divByX (a : PowerSeries K) (_ : a.constantCoeff = 0) : PowerSeries K :=
  PowerSeries.mk fun n => a.coeff (n + 1)
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:613-617 (PowerSeries.coeff_divByX)
/-- The coefficient of `x^n` in `a/x` is the coefficient of `x^{n+1}` in `a`. -/
@[simp]
theorem PowerSeries.coeff_divByX (a : PowerSeries K) (ha : a.constantCoeff = 0) (n : ŌäĢ) :
    (PowerSeries.divByX a ha).coeff n = a.coeff (n + 1) := by
  simp [PowerSeries.divByX]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:622-643 (fps_eq_X_mul_iff)
theorem fps_eq_X_mul_iff (a b : PowerSeries K) :
    a = PowerSeries.X * b Ōåö (a.constantCoeff = 0 Ōł¦
      Ōłā h : a.constantCoeff = 0, b = PowerSeries.divByX a h) := by
  constructor
  ┬Ę intro heq
    constructor
    ┬Ę simp [heq, PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]
    ┬Ę have hconst : a.constantCoeff = 0 := by simp [heq, PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]
      use hconst
      ext n
      rw [PowerSeries.coeff_divByX]
      rw [heq]
      simp [PowerSeries.coeff_succ_X_mul]
  ┬Ę intro Ō¤©hconst, h, heqŌ¤®
    rw [heq]
    ext n
    cases n with
    | zero =>
      simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_zero_eq_constantCoeff, hconst]
      simp [PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]
    | succ n =>
      simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_succ_X_mul, PowerSeries.coeff_divByX]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:647-650 (fps_X_mul_constantCoeff_zero)
@[simp]
theorem fps_X_mul_constantCoeff_zero (b : PowerSeries K) :
    (PowerSeries.X * b).constantCoeff = 0 := by
  simp [PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:661-664 (fps_divByX_X_mul)
theorem fps_divByX_X_mul (b : PowerSeries K) :
    PowerSeries.divByX (PowerSeries.X * b) (fps_X_mul_constantCoeff_zero b) = b := by
  ext n
  rw [PowerSeries.coeff_divByX, PowerSeries.coeff_succ_X_mul]
-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:669-678 (fps_exists_X_mul_of_constantCoeff_zero)
theorem fps_exists_X_mul_of_constantCoeff_zero (a : PowerSeries K) (ha : a.constantCoeff = 0) :
    Ōłā h : PowerSeries K, a = PowerSeries.X * h := by
  refine Ō¤©PowerSeries.divByX a ha, ?_Ō¤®
  ext n
  cases n with
  | zero =>
    simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_zero_eq_constantCoeff, ha]
    simp
  | succ n =>
    rw [PowerSeries.coeff_succ_X_mul, PowerSeries.coeff_divByX]",

  -- (1 - X)Ōü╗┬╣ = mk 1 (the power series with all coefficients 1)
  have h1 : (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)Ōü╗┬╣ = PowerSeries.mk 1 := by
    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ŌēĀ 0 := by simp
    symm
    rw [PowerSeries.eq_inv_iff_mul_eq_one hc]
    exact PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one F
  -- rescale (-1) maps (1 - X) to (1 + X)
  have h2 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) = 1 + PowerSeries.X := by
    ext n
    simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, map_sub, map_one, PowerSeries.coeff_one]
    cases n with
    | zero => simp
    | succ n =>
      simp only [Nat.succ_ne_zero, ŌåōreduceIte, PowerSeries.coeff_X]
      split_ifs with h
      ┬Ę simp [h]
      ┬Ę have hn : n ŌēĀ 0 := by omega
        simp [PowerSeries.coeff_X, hn]
  -- rescale preserves inverses (as a ring homomorphism)
  have h3 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) ((1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)Ōü╗┬╣) =
      (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X))Ōü╗┬╣ := by

    symm

        PowerSeries.inv_mul_cancel _ hc]
    simp
  -- Combine: (1+X)Ōü╗┬╣ = rescale(-1)((1-X)Ōü╗┬╣) = rescale(-1)(mk 1) = mk ((-1)^n)
  rw [ŌåÉ h2, ŌåÉ h3, h1]
  ext n
  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, PowerSeries.coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, mul_one]",

    simp
